qa_pairs.question: What types of IPv6 addresses are generally available within the Arbutus cloud environment, and what is the method used for setting up IP addresses on a Virtual Machine (VM) interface?
qa_pairs.answer: Within the Arbutus cloud, IPv6 Link-Local Addresses (LLA) and Global Unicast Addresses (GUA) are generally available. IP addresses are automatically set up on the VM interface using Stateless Address Auto Configuration (SLAAC).

qa_pairs.question: In the Arbutus cloud, what are the default security group rules for IPv6 Global Unicast Addresses (GUA), and what steps are required to allow traffic originating from outside a Virtual Machine?
qa_pairs.answer: By default, the security group rules in the Arbutus cloud allow all outbound traffic from a Virtual Machine (VM) via the IPv6 GUA. However, no traffic originating from outside the VM will be allowed until specific security group rules have been defined, which mirrors the behaviour of IPv4.

qa_pairs.question: How does one use the OpenStack command-line interface (CLI) to assign a new IPv6 network interface to an existing Virtual Machine in the Arbutus cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: To assign a new IPv6 network interface using the OpenStack CLI, first identify the Virtual Machine's ID by executing `openstack server list`. Once the ID is obtained, use the command `openstack server add network [VM_ID] IPv6-GUA`, replacing `[VM_ID]` with the specific ID of your Virtual Machine.
